cards.text: ...Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the parts of Libya belonging to Cyrene...
cards.connection: Places North Africa directly in the initial wave of the gospel's global expansion at Pentecost.
cards.cueContext: Shown when discussing the earliest origins of the African church.

cards.text: But there were some of them, men of Cyprus and Cyrene, who on coming to Antioch spoke to the Hellenists also, preaching the Lord Jesus.
cards.connection: Highlights North African believers as some of the first missionaries to preach the Gospel to Gentiles.
cards.cueContext: Used to show the active missionary role of early African Christians.

cards.text: And they compelled a passerby, Simon of Cyrene, who was coming in from the country, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to carry his cross.
cards.connection: A North African man literally bore the cross of Christ, foreshadowing the African church's role in bearing theological weight.
cards.cueContext: Shown when discussing the profound connection between North Africa and the crucifixion.

cards.text: Let both grow together until the harvest...
cards.connection: Augustine used the Parable of the Weeds to argue against the Donatists, asserting the church is a mixed body requiring grace.
cards.cueContext: Used during the discussion of the Donatist controversy.

cards.text: Let us walk properly as in the daytime, not in orgies and drunkenness... But put on the Lord Jesus Christ...
cards.connection: The exact verses Augustine read in the garden in Milan that led to his dramatic conversion.
cards.cueContext: Shown when describing Augustine's conversion experience.

cards.text: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
cards.connection: Directly contradicts the Arian heresy of the Vandals, affirming the full deity of Christ.
cards.cueContext: Used when explaining the theological danger of Arianism during the Vandal rule.
